answer:
We compute the total probability of a defective item:  
\(0.10 \times 0.001 + 0.20 \times 0.0005 + 0.30 \times 0.005 + 0.40 \times 0.002 = 0.0001 + 0.0001 + 0.0015 + 0.0008 = 0.0025\).  

Then using Bayes' theorem:  
P(A|defective) = \(0.0001 / 0.0025 = 1/25\),  
P(B|defective) = \(0.0001 / 0.0025 = 1/25\),  
P(C|defective) = \(0.0015 / 0.0025 = 3/5\),  
P(D|defective) = \(0.0008 / 0.0025 = 8/25\).  

This matches option E.
